title: Reopi

{{Quote|The desert world Reopi is home to one of the few remaining creatures in the Inner Sphere still hunted for its commercial value.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Reopi''' was a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]. It featured the vast equatorial area known as the [[Red Reopi Deserts]]. There, [[magus]] creatures [[Diet/Legends|fed]] on [[Insect/Legends|insects]] and [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] and was in turn preyed upon by the [[Reopi sand crusher]]s. Under strict government regulation, private Reopi businesses [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] magus for their [[Leather/Legends|hides]], which were prized off-world.

==Description==
{{Quote|Native to the vast equatorial sandy deserts of this world, the magus is a peaceful, solitary creature that feeds off insect and small reptilian life forms found in the Red Reopi Deserts.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Reopi was a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Creatures" /> located in the [[Reopi system/Legends|Reopi system]]. It was a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> Reopi was also situated within the region known as the [[Inner Sphere]]. It was the site of the [[Red Reopi Deserts]], which was a vast area of equatorial sandy deserts. The area was home to type of low-growing [[Plant/Legends|plant]]. The Red Reopi Deserts' wildlife included an abundance of [[Insect/Legends|insects]], among which was at least one winged form, as well as small [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] that inhabited desert oases. The burrowing creature known as the [[magus]] [[Diet/Legends|fed]] on the local insects and reptiles, and it in turn was preyed upon by the larger carnivore known as the [[Reopi sand crusher]].<ref name="Creatures" />

==History==
During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Reopi was mentioned in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of<ref name="Creatures" /> the planet<ref name="CSWE Chandrila">{{CSWECite|I|119|Chandrila}}</ref> [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to the magus.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Harvesting of magus hides is presently legal, though restricted solely to private Reopi businesses.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Reopi's native magus creature had a hide the toughness and durability of which was prized off-world, and, as a result, it was one of the few creatures in the Inner Sphere that were still [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] for their commercial value. However, strict government quota regulations protected the magus population, and the harvesting of their hides was restricted solely to private Reopi businesses. Magus hunters commonly utilized the presence of reptiles at the oases of the world's deserts as a lure for the creatures.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Reopi was mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The section of the book mentioning the celestial body was authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]], with Reopi being depicted in an illustration by [[Mike Vilardi]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Reopi [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square M-10.<ref name="TEA" />
